meta.description: Outbound alert. On a daily schedule, Hozi checks the LIVE engine output; if any district is projected HIGH (score >= 66) for September, it pushes a WhatsApp alert to the officer. This is the PUSH trigger — Hozi starts the conversation, unlike the inbound bot where the officer does. No secrets stored; set credentials at import.

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes: Runs every morning at 06:00. This is the PUSH trigger: Hozi initiates. Change the cron expression to alter the schedule, or swap this node for the full engine pipeline (Hozi/automation/hozi-pipeline.workflow.json) once live feeds are wired.

nodes.name: Fetch Projections
nodes.notes: Reads the LIVE engine output from the public Hozi app — the same file the map uses. This keeps the alert and the app in lockstep. In the VPS pilot this can instead read the freshly-computed data/projections.json from disk.

nodes.name: Find HIGH Districts + Build Alert
nodes.notes: Finds every HIGH-band district from the live data and builds one WhatsApp alert. Set the officer's number as the n8n environment variable HOZI_OFFICER_NUMBER (E.164 digits, e.g. 263771234567), or edit the placeholder here.

nodes.name: Any HIGH Districts?
nodes.notes: Only sends when at least one district is HIGH. On a quiet day the flow ends here — no alert, no noise.

nodes.name: Send WhatsApp Alert
nodes.notes: Sends the alert. Reuses the SAME WhatsApp Send credential and Phone Number ID as the inbound bot. IMPORTANT — WhatsApp rule: a business-INITIATED message outside the 24-hour customer-service window must use a pre-approved MESSAGE TEMPLATE, not free text. For the demo: have the officer message the bot first (opens the 24h window) and free text works. For the pilot: create an approved template (e.g. 'hozi_alert') and switch this node's operation to 'send template'. See SETUP guide.
